description: Rework runtime context assembly around a stable-prefix-first contract by separating non-persistent overlays from transcript state, making tool-profile changes sticky and explicit, aligning observability with the real request shape, and locking the behavior down with prefix-stability regressions.

userStories.title: Separate stable transcript state from turn-local overlays
userStories.description: Introduce an explicit prompt layering contract in the runtime so stable prefix items, turn-local overlays, and recent transcript tail are assembled through different paths. Move prompt-derived path guidance, tool-derived path guidance, execution charter text, and temporary runtime hints onto a non-persistent overlay channel that stays visible for the full `submitPrompt()` run but is dropped from cross-request history and resume state by default.
acceptanceCriteria: Runtime request assembly distinguishes `stable_prefix`, `turn_overlay`, and recent transcript/tail responsibilities instead of appending all runtime-authored items into a single cross-request history list.

Prompt-derived path guidance, tool-derived path guidance, execution charter text, and temporary recovery or verification hints are emitted as turn-local overlay items and are not appended to cross-request `QueryEngine.history` by default.
Overlay items remain visible across all provider turns within one `submitPrompt()` run, including tool-follow-up turns, but are absent from the next request unless regenerated for that request.

Session transcript persistence and `resumeFromTranscript()` do not depend on previously emitted overlay items, and resumed transcripts do not contain prompt-derived or tool-derived overlay items unless they were explicitly promoted into stable context.
Child/subagent requests are created from stable transcript state and regenerate overlay for the child turn instead of inheriting expired parent overlay items.
userStories.notes:
Source specs:

Files:
- src/runtime/query_engine.ts
- src/runtime/loop.ts
- src/context/service.ts
- src/runtime/subagents.ts
- src/runtime/items.ts
Verification:

- node --experimental-strip-types --test tests/e2e/e2e_query_engine_local.test.ts
- node --experimental-strip-types --test tests/e2e/e2e_subagent_local.test.ts
Notes:
- This story implements the layering contract first so later tool-profile and observability work can target a stable assembly shape.
- Do not reintroduce turn-local content into history under a renamed field.

userStories.title: Make tool schema decisions sticky within explicit profile epochs
userStories.description: Replace per-turn capability-profile churn with a sticky epoch model. Select and keep a tool schema for the active task epoch, switch only at explicit boundaries, and carry or rehydrate epoch state on resume so the runtime never silently changes schema because task state was lost between requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Within a single task/profile epoch, follow-up turns reuse the same tool schema set and ordering by default, so light prompt changes do not change the provider-visible tool schema hash.

Tool-schema changes happen only at explicit, low-frequency boundaries and each change records a bounded reason from `initial_epoch`, `user_correction`, `phase_switch`, `resume_rehydration`, or `safety_override` before the provider request is sent.

Session resume restores the prior profile epoch when possible or emits an explicit `resume_rehydration` schema-change event before the first resumed request instead of silently reclassifying from the new prompt alone.
Task control, execution charter semantics, mutation policy, and targeted registry narrowing from feature 106 remain in effect under the sticky epoch model.
userStories.notes:

- node --experimental-strip-types --test tests/e2e/e2e_session_resume.test.ts
Notes:
- Prefer schema stability first; use policy and mutation guards for finer-grained control before reaching for schema churn.
- Keep profile switching observable and reviewable rather than implicit.

userStories.title: Measure prompt stability from the actual assembled request
userStories.description: Update prompt observability so it reflects the exact request shape sent to the provider on each turn. Track the real tool schema, overlay placement, and stable-prefix boundaries from runtime assembly output instead of relying on runner-startup registry snapshots.
acceptanceCriteria: Prompt observability is recorded from the actual assembled provider request for each turn, including the real tool schema used on that turn rather than a startup-time default-registry estimate.
Per-turn observability includes `tool_schema_hash`, `schema_change_reason`, `overlay_tokens_estimate`, and stable-prefix metrics whose definition explicitly excludes turn-local overlay tokens.
When a profile epoch changes, observability can show both that the schema changed and the bounded reason for the change rather than only emitting a new hash.
Persisted usage or archive records keep prompt observability fields aligned with the real per-turn schema and token estimates instead of mixing initialization estimates with runtime values.
userStories.notes:

- node --experimental-strip-types --test tests/query_engine.test.ts
Notes:
- This story depends on the layering and sticky-epoch stories so observability can bind to the final request shape.
- Keep one source of truth for request-shape accounting.

userStories.title: Lock the stable-prefix contract with local and E2E regressions
userStories.description: Add regression coverage for the new context assembly rules across same-task follow-ups, tool-call continuations, session resume, and explicit phase/profile switches. The suite should prove that overlays do not leak into transcript state, schema churn is limited to explicit epoch changes, and cache-friendly prefix behavior from feature 107 is preserved.
acceptanceCriteria: Unit and local runtime tests cover overlay lifetime, overlay exclusion from history and resume state, and stable tool-schema behavior across same-task follow-up turns and tool-call continuations.
Cache and context-caching E2E tests continue to show a high stable-prefix ratio on follow-up turns and resumed turns after the new layering and epoch behavior lands.
A dedicated phase or profile switch E2E regression verifies that schema changes happen only at an explicit boundary and observability records the corresponding change reason.
Subagent local regression verifies that child requests receive stable transcript context plus child-specific regenerated overlay rather than stale parent overlay.
Feature 106 targeted narrowing and feature 107 cache-aware cost or prefix diagnostics remain covered and do not regress.
